column1:
- Simplify compound propositions using the laws of propositional logic.  
- Use the laws of propositional logic to prove logical equivalence between compound propositions.  
- Apply substitution of logically equivalent propositions within complex propositions.  
- Identify and apply key laws of propositional logic such as De Morgan’s laws, distributive, associative, commutative, identity, domination, complement, absorption, and conditional identities.  
- Construct formal proofs demonstrating logical equivalences and tautologies using propositional logic laws.
